mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations, directly reducing present values of future cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with persistently low federal funds rates creates a policy lag risk. Markets price in delayed aggressive tightening, causing negative forward returns over the next quarter as discount rates reset upward.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL is high and FEDFUNDS is low, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during recovery phases.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in > 60% of months where INDPRO rises during identified recovery regimes, or the mechanism shows no predictive alpha in regime-filtered walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real estate sales indicate robust household balance sheets and consumer confidence, which translates into higher retail and service sector revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS momentum remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns do not significantly outperform the baseline when RSAFS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in total nonfarm payrolls and a rise in the unemployment rate confirms a broad labor market contraction. This dual deterioration forces equity repricing downward, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts positive returns in > 55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect between PAYEMS and UNRATE is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample models.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, it confirms entrenched high inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 240, or the threshold signal fails to produce a significant negative Rank IC in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts signals strong construction sector activity and upstream supply chain demand, which drives positive earnings revisions for materials and industrials, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in > 65% of months where HOUST rises during late-cycle regimes, or the housing starts signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in regime-specific out-of-sample tests.
